issues.blurb: Camper-van builds lean hard on the house battery, solar, and DC-DC charging, and these systems are a frequent source of dead-battery and charging faults.
Compact fresh, gray, and water-pump plumbing leaks, loses prime, or freezes in cold weather because there is little room to insulate it.
The small sealed cabin traps moisture from cooking and breathing, leading to condensation, window fogging, and mildew without active ventilation.
Cabinetry, panels, and bolted-in conversion components work loose over rough roads and produce persistent rattles and squeaks.
Furnace or diesel/propane cabin heaters fail to ignite or cycle improperly, a real problem in a van relied on for cold-weather camping.
Small fresh, gray, and black tanks fill or empty quickly, so owners report frequent dumping and short off-grid range as a recurring limitation.

narrative.headline: What Class B camper van owners report most
narrative.summary: These are issues commonly reported by Class B owners as a general reference, not expert-verified data and possibly outdated. Because these vans are small, the house electrical, water system, and condensation tend to dominate what owners mention.

issues.title: House Battery, Electrical & Solar
issues.blurb: Commonly reported (~26%) — van builds lean on house battery, solar, and DC-DC charging, a frequent fault source.

issues.title: Water System & Pump
issues.blurb: Commonly reported (~22%) — compact plumbing leaks, loses prime, or freezes with little room to insulate.

issues.title: Ventilation & Condensation
issues.blurb: Often reported (~20%) — the small sealed cabin traps moisture, causing fogging and mildew without airflow.

issues.title: Conversion Fit & Finish Rattles
issues.blurb: Reported (~16%) — cabinetry and bolted-in components loosen on rough roads and rattle.

issues.title: Heater
issues.blurb: Reported (~10%) — cabin heaters fail to ignite or cycle properly, a problem for cold-weather camping.

issues.title: Limited Tankage
issues.blurb: Reported (~6%) — small tanks fill or empty fast, so owners note frequent dumping and short off-grid range.
